rules.title: Link must use native anchor element with href
rules.description: The .link class must be applied to an <a> element with a non-empty href attribute. An <a> without href is not keyboard-focusable and does not have the link role.
rules.expected: element is <a> AND has non-empty href attribute
rules.message: Link is missing href attribute or is not an <a> element. Add href to make the link keyboard operable.
rules.fix: Ensure the element is <a href="/destination" class="link ...">. If no destination exists yet, use href="#" with aria-current or a placeholder.

rules.title: Disabled link must have aria-disabled='true'
rules.description: When a link is visually disabled (.link with pointer-events:none or cursor:not-allowed applied), it must also carry aria-disabled='true' so screen readers can communicate the disabled state.
rules.message: Disabled link has aria-disabled but the value is not 'true'. Use aria-disabled="true".
rules.fix: Set aria-disabled="true" on the <a> element. Pair with tabindex="-1" and a JavaScript click guard.

rules.title: Disabled link must have tabindex='-1'
rules.description: A link with aria-disabled='true' must also have tabindex='-1' to remove it from the keyboard tab order. Without this, screen reader users can still tab to the link and may attempt to activate it.
rules.message: Disabled link (.link[aria-disabled='true']) is missing tabindex='-1'. Keyboard users can still reach and activate this link.
rules.fix: Add tabindex="-1" to the disabled <a> element.

rules.title: Disabled link should have JavaScript click guard
rules.description: CSS pointer-events:none prevents mouse clicks on a disabled link but does not prevent keyboard Enter activation. A JavaScript event listener that calls event.preventDefault() when aria-disabled='true' is set is required to fully disable keyboard interaction.
rules.expected: element has data-js-guarded attribute OR surrounding implementation provides click prevention

check.note: Cannot be validated via static analysis. Verify in runtime testing.

rules.message: Disabled link may still be activatable via keyboard. Verify that a JavaScript click guard (event.preventDefault() when aria-disabled='true') is applied.

rules.title: External link (target=_blank) must communicate new-tab behavior
rules.description: A link that opens in a new tab (target='_blank') must inform screen reader users. This can be done via a visually hidden (opens in new tab) or via aria-label that includes the destination description.
rules.expected: element has aria-label containing 'new tab' OR contains .sr-only with 'new tab' text

conditions: element aria-label contains 'new tab' or 'opens in'
element contains child with sr-only class containing 'new tab' or 'opens in'

rules.message: Link with target='_blank' does not communicate that it opens in a new tab. Screen reader users will not be warned.
rules.fix: Add (opens in new tab) inside the link, or add an aria-label that includes the destination and '(opens in new tab)'.

rules.title: Focus ring must not be suppressed
rules.description: The link component defines a visible focus ring via outline on :focus-visible. Applying outline:none or outline:0 on the .link element or any :focus selector will remove the visible focus indicator and fail WCAG 2.4.7.
rules.expected: no inline style or selector sets outline:none or outline:0 on the element
rules.message: Focus ring is suppressed on this link. Remove outline:none to restore keyboard focus visibility.
rules.fix: Remove any outline:none or outline:0 rule from .link, .link:focus, .link:focus-visible, or inline styles.

rules.title: Link text must not be generic
rules.description: Generic link text like 'click here', 'read more', 'here', or 'link' does not describe the destination when read out of context by a screen reader (e.g. in a links list).
rules.expected: text content is not a generic navigation phrase
rules.message: Link text is generic and does not describe the destination. Screen reader users navigating by links list cannot determine the link purpose.
rules.fix: Replace generic text with a descriptive label, e.g. 'Read the Privacy Policy' instead of 'Read more'.

rules.title: Link root element must be <a>
rules.description: The .link class must only be applied to <a> elements. Applying it to <div>, <span>, or <button> elements creates accessibility and semantic issues.
rules.message: The .link class is applied to a non-anchor element. Only <a> elements should receive this class.
rules.fix: Change the element to <a class="link ..."> and add a valid href attribute.

rules.title: Link color must use design tokens, not hardcoded values
rules.description: Link color must be sourced from --Link-link-* or --Global-* CSS custom properties. Hardcoded hex, rgb, or rgba values bypass the token system and will not update with theme changes.
rules.expected: color property uses var(--Link-*) or var(--Global-*) or inherits from component internal variables
rules.message: Link has hardcoded color value. Replace with the appropriate --Link-link-* token.
rules.fix: Replace hardcoded color with the correct token from reference.json style_tokens.
